Article Title: 'Main bhi cockroach': Student protests against NEET reach New York, London - watch
Article Text:
Protests spread to London

Protest continues at Jantar Mantar

Cockroach Janata Party (CJP)

NEW DELHI: New York witnessed demonstrations in support of 'cockroaches', on Tuesday, as students and members of Indian diaspora rallied over alleged irregularities in the NEET examinations.The protesters gathered at the Mahatma Gandhi statue in New York City's Union Square carrying placards in support of students.Similar solidarity demonstrations were held in London, where protesters gathered outside the Indian High Commission in support of the nationwide movement demanding accountability over the alleged NEET irregularities and reforms in India's education system.Holding placards and raising slogans, the demonstrators expressed support and called for greater transparency, accountability and reforms in the country's examination and education system.Manchester, Berlin and Netherlands also witnessed protest demading Dharmendra Pradhan's resignation.Jantar Mantar continues to witness thousands of protesters on Wesneday days after the 'Sansad Chalo' march on July 20, as the agitation over alleged irregularities in the NEET examinations showed no signs of slowing down.On Tuesday, thecalled off its proposed sit-in outside the Delhi Police headquarters after claiming that all protesters detained following the march had been released. The outfit had earlier alleged that hundreds of its supporters were being held in custody and denied access to legal counsel, a charge that was rejected by the Delhi Police.Meanwhile, opposition parties have extended support to Wangchuk's agitation and staged protests in the national capital, demanding accountability over the alleged irregularities in the conduct of the NEET examination.
Article Summary: Students and members of Indian diaspora rallied over alleged irregularities in the NEET examinations. Similar solidarity demonstrations were held in London, Manchester, Berlin and Netherlands. Jantar Mantar continues to witness thousands of protesters days after the 'Sansad Chalo' march on July 20.

Article Title: 'Picked up in car, sexually assaulted, filmed': Bengal woman 'gang raped' at Karnataka hotel; 3 arrested
Article Text:
Mysuru police have detained three youths from T Narasipura in connection with an alleged gang rape (Representative image).

(The victim's identity has not been revealed to protect her privacy as per Supreme court directives on cases related to sexual assault)

MYSURU: Mysuru district police arrested three youths from T Narasipura in connection with an alleged gang rape of a woman in a lodge room in Kollegala, Chamarajanagar district, last week.The woman, stated to be from West Bengal, has been moved to a rehabilitation centre for counselling and support, and her identity is being verified.Superintendent of police Mallikarjun Baladandi said a gang-rape case has been registered under the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita (BNS) based on the survivor’s complaint, and the three accused—Rihan, Kiran and Kishore—have been arrested.According to the complaint, the accused picked her up in a car, took her to a hotel in the neighbouring district, sexually assaulted her in hotel room and then attempted to extort money from her.She reportedly escaped after promising to pay them money kept at home. She got out of the car on reaching her residence, locked herself inside and raised an alarm, after which neighbours alerted police.Police, who are doubting the veracity of the complaint, said preliminary inquiry indicates the woman voluntarily travelled with the accused to the hotel. Investigators also claim a dispute arose after the accused found she had filmed a private act on her phone. The accused allegedly seized her mobile phone and formatted the phone and then demanded Rs 1 lakh from her.Though incident of rape is said to have happened in Chamarajanagar limits, Mysuru police have registered a case as the woman was allegedly picked up from a village in T Narasipura.
Article Summary: Mysuru district police arrested three youths from T Narasipura in connection with an alleged gang rape of a woman. The woman, stated to be from West Bengal, has been moved to a rehabilitation centre for counselling and support. The victim's identity has not been revealed to protect her privacy as per Supreme court directives on cases related to sexual assault.

Article Title: Health minister JP Nadda, MoS Jitendra Singh meet Sonam Wangchuk at Medanta Hospital: Report
Article Text:
JP Nadda and minister of state for the Prime Minister's Office, Dr Jitendra Singh met Sonam Wangchuk at Medanta hospital

NEW DELHI: Union minister Jagat Prakash Nadda, minister of state for the Prime Minister's office Dr Jitendra Singh went to Medanta Hospital in Gurugram and met Activist Sonam Wangchuk to enquire about his health, as per PTI sources.This came after the Delhi high court on Tuesday ordered the immediate transfer of scientist and activist Sonam Wangchuk from Safdarjung Hospital to Medanta Hospital in Gurugram amid his prolonged hunger strike over the alleged NEET paper leak.The division bench directed that Wangchuk be treated by doctors at Medanta as per established medical protocol and said he must follow the medical advice of the treating team after his transfer.The order came after Wangchuk's wife, Gitanjali Angmo, challenged a single judge's decision refusing permission to shift him from Safdarjung Hospital.During the hearing, the Centre informed the high court that it had "no objection" to Wangchuk being shifted to Medanta Hospital. Solicitor General Tushar Mehta, however, urged that Wangchuk "remain under continuous medical supervision" and "not be discharged except on the advice of Medanta's doctors."Wangchuk entered the 24th day of his hunger strike on Tuesday as the Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) stepped up its protest against the Centre over the alleged NEET paper leak and renewed its demand for Union education minister Dharmendra Pradhan's resignation. Protesters also continued their sit-in at Delhi's Jantar Mantar.
Article Summary: Union minister Jagat Prakash Nadda, minister of state for the Prime Minister's office Dr Jitendra Singh went to Medanta Hospital in Gurugram to enquire about his health. This came after the Delhi high court on Tuesday ordered the immediate transfer of scientist and activist Sonam Wangchuk from Safdarjung Hospital. Wangchuk entered the 24th day of his hunger strike on Tuesday.

Article Title: Modi government picks a Sikh again to head minorities panel
Article Text:
Harjit Singh Grewal is the second Sikh to head the body under the Modi govt after Iqbal Singh Lalpura

NEW DELHI: After remaining without a chief for 15 months, National Commission for Minorities (NCM) Tuesday saw its new chairman Harjit Singh Grewal, assume charge. He is the second Sikh to head the body under the Modi govt after Iqbal Singh Lalpura. The post had been vacant since April 2025, when Lalpura's three-year term ended.Before Lalpura and Grewal, the NCM had largely been headed by members of the Muslim community since its inception in 1993, except between 2003 and 2006, when Tarlochan Singh was chairperson. Syed Ghayorul Hasan Rizvi was NCM chief from May 2017 to 2020. He was preceded by Naseem Ahmad (2014-2017).Grewal's appointment gains significance as it comes ahead of 2027 Punjab assembly polls. A senior BJP functionary and national executive committee member from Punjab, Grewal is regarded as one of the most vocal Sikh voices in the state.A notification on Grewal's appointment was issued by ministry of minority affairs on July 16. He will serve a three-year term from the date he assumed office, or until further orders, whichever is earlier.The ministry said his appointment marked "the beginning of a renewed commitment towards promoting the welfare, inclusion, empowerment and socio-economic development of minority communities."Muslim representative S. Munawari Begum took charge as NCM vice-chairperson, while Glenn Souza Ticlo joined as the Christian representative. Parsi representative Berjis Desai joined the commission in March.The NCM now has a chairperson and three members, with three posts still vacant.
Article Summary: Harjit Singh Grewal is the second Sikh to head the body under the Modi govt. The post had been vacant since April 2025, when Lalpura's three-year term ended. The NCM had largely been headed by members of the Muslim community since its inception in 1993.

Article Title: Another SC judge, the 3rd, opts out of hearing Gadling bail plea
Article Text: New Delhi: Supreme Court judge Shree Chandrashekhar on Tuesday recused himself from hearing the bail plea of lawyer-activist Surendra Gadling in the 2016 Gadchiroli arson case. He is the third judge to recuse himself in the case after Justice Atul Chandurkar and Justice MM Sundresh, reports Amit Anand Choudhary.At the outset of the hearing, which was listed before a bench of Justices Prashant Kumar Mishra and Shree Chandrashekhar, the court asked for the matter to be listed before another bench."It will go to some other combination; my brother has some difficulty. Post before the bench in which one of us isn't there," Justice Mishra said.Gadling had approached the apex court challenging the Bombay high court order denying him bail in the arson case. He has been booked under various sections of UAPA and IPC for allegedly being part of a conspiracy by Maoists to set fire to more than 80 vehicles transporting iron ore from Surjagarh mines in Maharashtra's Gadchiroli.
Article Summary: Supreme Court judge Shree Chandrashekhar recused himself from hearing the bail plea of lawyer-activist Surendra Gadling in the 2016 Gadchiroli arson case. He is the third judge to recuse himself in the case after Justice Atul Chandurkar and Justice MM Sundresh.

Article Title: Rahul dragged, Priyanka clings to bus door: Five pictures that defined Congress protest outside PM Modi's residence
Article Text:
Rahul Gandhi being taken away by police

Mallikarjun Kharge

Rahul Gandhi

Rahul Gandhi, Jitendra Singh, Govind Mohan

Rahul Gandhi

Rahul Gandhi

Priyanka Gandhi Vadra

NEW DELHI: High drama unfolded outside Prime Minister Narendra Modi's residence in Delhi on Tuesday as the Congress, led by Rahul Gandhi, arrived to stage a sit-in protest, catching security personnel off guard.The Congress leaders demanded the resignation of PM Modi, Union home minister Amit Shah, and education minister Dharmendra Pradhan.Rahul Gandhi also appealed to the public to join what was planned as an indefinite protest, following which leaders from other opposition parties joined the demonstration.Congress leaders tied plastic bands around their hands and raised slogans, protesting the government's action.The government sent Union minister Jitendra Singh and home secretary Govind Mohan to urge Rahul Gandhi and the Congress to call off the protest, citing the high-security nature of the area. However, the request was rejected.Singh claimed that he informed Rahul Gandhi that the government had agreed to discuss the NEET paper leak issue in Parliament, following which the Congress MP "changed the goalpost" and demanded the resignation of Dharmendra Pradhan.Eventually, Delhi Police detained Rahul Gandhi and other leaders participating in the protest.The Congress claimed that Rahul Gandhi sustained a nose injury while in Delhi Police custody.Meanwhile, party MP Priyanka Gandhi Vadra attempted to resist her detention by clinging to the door of a bus in which protesters were being taken away.The dramatic protest came a day after Delhi Police and other security forces stopped a march by the Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) to Parliament, with protesters demanding the resignation of Dharmendra Pradhan over the NEET (UG) paper leak.The CJP march, held on the first day of the Monsoon Session of Parliament, turned violent, leaving both protesters and security personnel injured.
Article Summary: Congress leaders stage sit-in protest outside Prime Minister Narendra Modi's residence in Delhi. They demand resignation of PM Modi, home minister Amit Shah, and education minister Dharmendra Pradhan. Delhi Police detain Rahul Gandhi and other leaders participating in the protest.

Article Title: 'Nadir of Indians' integrity': Allahabad HC judge slams Ram Mandir theft, backs death penalty
Article Text:
Allahabad High Court on Ram Temple theft (ANI image on right)

Backs death penalty

Supreme Court orders fresh SIT probe

The case so far

NEW DELHI: Allahabad High Court judge, on Tuesday, made strong observations on the alleged embezzlement of donations at the Ram temple in Ayodhya, saying the incident reflected the"nadir of Indians' integrity"and that those unfazed by it could not be shamed.The observations were made while the bench was hearing a matter related to the Uttar Pradesh government's recent bulldozer actions. During the hearing, Justice Sreedharan criticised the deep-rooted corruption in public institutions.The judge also advocated the death penalty for offences under the Prevention of Corruption Act.Justice Atul Sreedharan said, "The recent controversy relating to the theft of donations at the Ram temple is the proverbial last straw on the camel's back. Nothing can shame people who remain unfazed by the theft at the Ram temple which epitomises the nadir of Indians' integrity".""The average Indian has accepted corruption as normal. He does not consider it wrong unless someone is caught. India ranks 91st out of 182 countries on the Transparency list, and even that does not make us feel ashamed," he added.The Supreme Court earlier has directed the constitution of a fresh Special Investigation Team (SIT) to probe the alleged Ram Mandir donation theft case.A bench headed by Chief Justice of India Surya Kant said the new SIT should be led by a senior IPS officer to ensure a fair and conclusive investigation."We only want to issue a word of caution. Do not politicise this matter. At first glance, this appears to be an offence under criminal law. It must be investigated fairly, independently and with complete honesty. The investigation should be taken to its logical conclusion," the CJI observed.The controversy erupted on June 7 after former Samajwadi Party minister Pawan Pandey alleged that donations worth Rs 5 crore to Rs 7.5 crore had been stolen from the Ram Mandir.Samajwadi Party chief Akhilesh Yadav questioned the government's silence and sought a judicial probe, while Ram Mandir Trust general secretary Champat Rai rejected the allegations. BJP leader Dr Rajneesh Singh later demanded a CBI investigation, following which the Prime Minister's Office sought a report from the temple trust.An FIR was registered on June 25 on the complaint of trustee Krishna Mohan. Police has subsequently arrested eight accused in the matter till now.
Article Summary: Allahabad High Court judge criticises deep-rooted corruption in public institutions. Justice Atul Sreedharan advocates death penalty for offences under Prevention of Corruption Act. Supreme Court orders constitution of a fresh Special Investigation Team (SIT) to probe alleged Ram Mandir donation theft case.

Article Title: 'Have some shame, spare us bhashans': Mahua Moitra hits back at Dharmendra Pradhan over students remark
Article Text:
Trinamool Congress MP Mahua Moitra took a swipe at Union education minister Dharmendra Pradhan after he accused the Congress of exploiting students for politics.

NEW DELHI: Trinamool Congress MP Mahua Moitra on Wednesday took a swipe at Union education minister Dharmendra Pradhan after he accused the Congress of exploiting students for politics, saying the only thing the government should be "committed to delivering" is his resignation.Pradhan, through a post on X, defended the Centre's handling of the examination controversy and criticised the Congress for staging a protest outside Prime Minister Narendra Modi's residence.To this, Moitra responded, "With all due respect have some shame & spare us the bhashans. The only thing your government should be committed to delivering right now is your resignation on a plate. Thank you."Earlier, Pradhan had accused LoP Rahul Gandhi and the Congress of "shamelessly exploit(ing) students as political tools to manufacture disruption during the Monsoon Session of Parliament."He alleged that despite the government's willingness to hold a discussion on the issue, the Congress had chosen "political spectacle over democratic debate"."We owe our students more than outrage. We owe them answers, reforms and accountability. That is exactly what our government remains committed to delivering," Pradhan said.His remarks came after the Congress staged a protest over the alleged examination irregularities, while the Cockroach Janta Party's ( CJP ) agitation over examination reforms continued.Activist Sonam Wangchuk continued his indefinite hunger strike, and Delhi Police registered an FIR in connection with Monday's clashes during the CJP's 'Sansad Chalo' march.
Article Summary: Trinamool Congress MP Mahua Moitra took a swipe at Union education minister Dharmendra Pradhan after he accused the Congress of exploiting students for politics. He said the only thing the government should be "committed to delivering" is his resignation.

Article Title: 7 FIRs filed for theft of 1,750kg copper coils from MSEDCL transformers in Panvel
Article Text:
The thefts were allegedly committed between April and July 6. (AI-generated image)

NAVI MUMBAI: Panvel City Police have registered seven separate FIRs against unidentified persons for allegedly stealing 1,750 kg of copper coils worth Rs 8.75 lakh from MSEDCL transformers kept at different plots in Karanjade node of Panvel taluka. The thefts were allegedly committed between April and July 6. The incident came to light after MSEDCL officials inspected the plots.According to the complainant, Amit Palve (42), assistant engineer with MSEDCL's Pargaon branch in Panvel taluka, the thieves broke open the windows of rooms at seven different plots where the defunct transformers were kept and stole the copper coils from them.When contacted, Palve said, "The seven transformers were defunct and hence kept in the rooms at the respective plots. On July 6, the theft came to light and we informed the Panvel City Police, who carried out a preliminary investigation. As there was no CCTV surveillance in the area, there was no technical evidence. Since it was a major theft, an FIR was registered against unidentified accused under relevant BNS sections for the offences of trespassing and theft. "
Article Summary: Panvel City Police have registered seven separate FIRs against unidentified persons for allegedly stealing 1,750 kg of copper coils worth Rs 8.75 lakh. The thefts were allegedly committed between April and July 6. The incident came to light after MSEDCL officials inspected the plots.

Article Title: Man dies after jumping from 11th-floor terrace of Kondhwa residential building
Article Text:
Pune:

A 30-year-old man allegedly died by suicide after jumping from the terrace of the 11th floor of a residential building in Kondhwa at around noon on Monday.Kondhwa police have registered an accidental death report (ADR).Officer Santosh Khetmalas said, “We studied the sequence of the incident and also spoke to his family members. We suspect it is a case of suicide. Although no suicide note was found at his residence.”The deceased cut the iron mesh installed near the terrace of the building. He lived on the same floor. After cutting it, he created enough space to jump. He had been unemployed for the past six months and was under tremendous stress. He is survived by his wife, a private school teacher and their seven-year-old son,” Khetmalas added.
Article Summary: The deceased cut the iron mesh installed near the terrace of the building. He had been unemployed for the past six months and was under tremendous stress. He is survived by his wife, a private school teacher and their seven-year-old son.

Article Title: Shortage of supporters forces Sena (UBT), NCP (SP) to join agitation by others: Mahajan
Article Text:
Mahajan was in Nashik to review Kumbh Mela-related infrastructure and administrative works ahead of the mega religious gathering

Nashik

:

Water resources and Kumbh Mela minister Girish Mahajan on Sunday took a swipe at Shiv Sena (UBT) and NCP (SP), claiming the two opposition parties were increasingly participating in agitations organised by others because they were unable to mobilise supporters on their own.Responding to questions on protests in support of Sonam Wangchuk and the backing they have received from opposition parties, Mahajan said, “The picture is very clear. The two parties of Maha Vikas Aghadi do not have supporters of their own. Whenever they call for an agitation, they are unable to gather a crowd for their programmes. I think the only option they have left with is to join the agitations called by others to try and get some solace and support,” Mahajan said.The minister further claimed that while the opposition succeeds in generating publicity around protests, it has failed to attract mass participation.Asked about the escalation of protests in New Delhi and reports of police action against protesting youth, whose grievances were initially ignored, Mahajan said, “I do not have the detailed information about what exactly has happened there.”Meanwhile, commenting on preparations for the upcoming Kumbh Mela in Nashik, Mahajan acknowledged delays in some projects. “The works were slightly delayed. I conducted review meeting of works. I have asked the officials to pick up the pace,” he said.Mahajan was in Nashik to review Kumbh Mela-related infrastructure and administrative works ahead of the mega religious gathering.
Article Summary: Mahajan was in Nashik to review Kumbh Mela-related infrastructure and administrative works ahead of the mega religious gathering. He claimed Shiv Sena and NCP were increasingly participating in agitations organised by others because they were unable to mobilise supporters on their own.

Article Title: TN and Pondy bar council election results declared
Article Text:
Election results

Chennai:

After 86 days of voting, the results of election conducted to elect 23 members to the Bar Council of Tamil Nadu and Puducherry was declared by the Supreme Court-appointed high-power committee on Tuesday.Those who elected include former chairman of the council P S Amalraj, and 17 others including advocates K Balu, R C Paul Kanagaraj, M Velmurugan, G Mohanakrishnan, T Prasanna.The election to choose 23 members to the council was held on April 30 across Tamil Nadu and Puducherry. A total of 143 advocates contested the election. Polling was conducted at 168 polling stations, 166 in Tamil Nadu and two in Puducherry. The election recorded a voter turnout of 81%.The counting of votes cast under preferential voting system commenced in the first week of May. As per the election rules, a candidate securing 3,001 first-preference votes is declared elected. The newly elected members will elect the chairperson, vice chairperson and heads of various committees of the council.
Article Summary: The election to choose 23 members to the council was held on April 30 across Tamil Nadu and Puducherry. A total of 143 advocates contested the election. The newly elected members will elect the chairperson, vice chairperson and heads of various committees of the council.

Article Title: Man dies after being attacked by wife near Coimbatore
Article Text: COIMBATORE: A 53-year-old vegetable seller died in Coimbatore Medical College Hospital on Monday night after he was assaulted by his wife during a drunken quarrel near Sirumugai in Coimbatore district.The deceased has been identified as K Chenniappan, 53, of Periyur in Palathottam. He was selling vegetables on a pushcart. His wife, Pushpalatha, 47, also sold vegetables using a pushcart. The couple have two sons.According to police sources, on July 17 night, Chenniappan, who was in an inebriated state, went to his wife’s vegetable pushcart, picked up a fight with her and assaulted her. Enraged, Pushpalatha picked up a log from the ground and struck him on the head.Chenniappan, who sustained severe head injuries, was rushed to Mettupalayam govt hospital. Later, he transferred to Coimbatore Medical College Hospital for further treatment. However, he died on Monday night without responding to treatment.The Sirumugai police registered a case and arrested Pushpalatha.
Article Summary: The deceased has been identified as K Chenniappan, 53, of Periyur in Palathottam. He was selling vegetables on a pushcart. His wife, Pushpalatha, 47, also sold vegetables using a push cart. The couple have two sons.

Article Title: Two men arrested in connection with coworker’s death near Coimbatore
Article Text:
Representative image

COIMBATORE: The Sirumugai police in Coimbatore district have arrested two guest workers in connection with the death of a 27-year-old man who was their coworker.The deceased has been identified as Pisi Yadav, 27, from Bihar, who was employed at a private firm in Jadayampalayam near Sirumugai.Police initially registered a case under Section 194 of the BNSS for suspicious death, based on a complaint lodged by the victim’s brother, Roshan Kumar, 21.According to police, Pisi Yadav was involved in a quarrel with his co-workers on July 16. He was later admitted to govt hospital in Mettupalayam, with claims that he had sustained injuries in a road accident. On July 18, Roshan Kumar was informed that his brother had died without responding to treatment. He lodged a complaint with the Sirumugai police on July 18.During investigations, police questioned two of the deceased’s co-workers, Ujith, 43, and Ajith Jamadar, 38. Both are natives of Bihar. Inquiries revealed that the suspects allegedly assaulted Pisi Yadav in their residential quarters in the early hours of July 17 following a dispute over repayment of money. He was later taken to hospital, where he succumbed to his injuries.Based on the evidence collected, police altered the case on July 19 from suspicious death under Section 194 BNSS to Section 105 (culpable homicide not amounting to murder) of the BNS. Both were remanded in judicial custody.
Article Summary: The deceased has been identified as Pisi Yadav, 27, from Bihar. He was employed at a private firm in Jadayampalayam near Sirumugai. Police questioned two of the deceased’s co-workers, Ujith, 43, and Ajith Jamadar, 38.

Article Title: Sengottaiyan flays previous DMK govt for not having pursued legal remedies on Mekedatu dam issue
Article Text:
K A Sengottaiyan





ERODE: Tamil Nadu revenue minister K A Sengottaiyan on Saturday criticized the previous DMK govt for not having pursued legal remedies to stop Karnataka’s move to build a dam across the Cauvery river in Mekedatu.Speaking at a consultation meeting of officials from different departments held at the Erode district collectorate, Sengottaiyan reiterated that chief minister C Joseph Vijay had urged Prime Minister Narendra Modi to halt the move to build a dam in Mekedatu.During the review meeting, the minister told officials to deliver results quickly, saying the govt’s performance since coming to power warranted a thorough review and that the people’s demands should be met without delay. “Officers must complete necessary office procedures promptly and ensure citizens receive timely relief,” he told reporters after the meeting.The review, chaired by district collector S Kandasamy and also attended by handloom minister M Vijay Balaji, covered a wide range of administrative concerns.A major focus was the Athikadavu-Avinashi integrated drinking water project. Officials were directed to find out why more than 70 ponds were not getting filled and asked them to take remedial measures. Officials were instructed to begin silt-removal operations in the Bhavanisagar Dam to improve storage capacity and downstream supply.On allegations that liquor outlets were operating in violation of regulations, the revenue minister demanded concrete statistics and warned that strict action would follow where breaches were confirmed.
Article Summary: K A Sengottaiyan criticized the previous DMK govt for not having pursued legal remedies to stop Karnataka’s move to build a dam across the Cauvery river in Mekedatu. He reiterated that chief minister C Joseph Vijay had urged Prime Minister Narendra Modi to halt the move.

Article Text:
DMK Deputy General Secretary and Tuticorin MP Kanimozhi Karunanidhi visited Vilathikulam DMK MLA Markandayan at the Palayamkottai Central Prison on Tuesday

Tirunelveli: DMK deputy general secretary and Tuticorin MP K Kanimozhi met Vilathikulam MLA G V Markandayan, who is lodged in Palayamkottai central prison in a case registered over his alleged remarks against chief minister C Joseph Vijay at a public meeting at Kovilpatti.Markandayan was booked under three sections following a complaint stating he made inflammatory and threatening remarks against the CM at the public meeting on July 18. Kanimozhi enquired about the MLA’s well-being at the prison where he is remanded in judicial custody. District secretaries R Avudaiyappan and Geetha Jeevan, MLAs Anitha R Radhakrishnan, Abdul Wahab and K Karunanidhi, as well as Tuticorin mayor Jegan Periyasamy accompanied the MP.Kanimozhi told reporters that Palayamkottai prison is nothing new to DMK. “Party members view imprisonment here with pride because it is the same jail where M Karunanidhi was once held. Similarly, he (Markandayan) too has accepted his imprisonment with a sense of pride,” she said.
Article Summary: DMK deputy general secretary and Tuticorin MP K Kanimozhi met Vilathikulam DMK MLA G V Markandayan at the Palayamkottai Central Prison on Tuesday. Mark andayan was booked under three sections following a complaint stating he made inflammatory and threatening remarks against the CM at a public meeting.

Article Title: Kerala HC directs Palarivattom police to provide protection to viral Kumbh Mela girl
Article Text:
Justice Bechu Kurian Thomas issued the interim order after suo motu impleading the SHO in the matter and adjourned the petition to July 28

Kochi

:

High court on Tuesday directed Palarivattom police SHO to provide adequate police protection to the ‘viral Kumbh Mela girl’ from Madhya Pradesh in a petition filed by her seeking protection, alleging threats to her life.Justice Bechu Kurian Thomas issued the interim order after suo motu impleading the SHO in the matter and adjourned the petition to July 28. The girl had approached HC seeking police protection, alleging that several social media handles had begun propagating hate campaigns against her and that she had received threatening text messages from unknown persons because she had married a film actor from Madhya Pradesh. She further stated that their marriage was solemnised at a temple in Thiruvananthapuram and was subsequently registered under Special Marriage Act on March 11. Meanwhile, the Madhya Pradesh police registered a case based on a complaint by the girl’s father alleging that his daughter was abducted and that she was a minor at the time of her marriage.At an earlier hearing, HC directed Ernakulam Central police to provide her with adequate protection.However, the police later informed court that they had been unable to locate her at the address she had provided or contact her using the phone number she had furnished. Accordingly, HC on July 10 vacated its interim order granting her police protection. When the petition came up for consideration on Tuesday, the govt pleader submitted that the girl had approached Palarivattom police seeking protection, as she was currently residing within its jurisdiction. Police had provided her with the mobile number of an additional sub inspector to contact in case of an emergency.The girl’s counsel submitted that, following police verification pursuant to her earlier request for protection, the owner of the house where she had been residing asked her to vacate the premises. Consequently, she shifted her residence to an area. Upon these submissions, HC suo motu impleaded the Palarivattom SHO and directed him to provide her with adequate police protection.
Article Summary: High court on Tuesday directed Palarivattom police SHO to provide adequate police protection to the ‘viral Kumbh Mela girl’ from Madhya Pradesh. Justice Bechu Kurian Thomas issued the interim order after suo motu impleading the SHO in the matter. The girl had approached HC seeking police protection, alleging that several social media handles had begun propagating hate campaigns against her.

Article Title: Kerala HC dismisses plea by sexual assault survivor to re-register FIR against cops
Article Text:
Kochi

:

High court has dismissed as withdrawn a petition filed by the survivor in a sexual assault case against three senior police officers in Malappuram, seeking to cancel and re-register the FIR by including the officers’ names as accused instead of merely referring to their official positions, and by invoking Section 34 of the IPC.Justice Jobin Sebastian, while considering the petition on Tuesday, orally observed that a case had already been registered in the alleged incident pursuant to the magistrate’s direction and that further details could emerge during the investigation. At this stage, the prayer to invoke Section 34 of the IPC, which deals with acts done by several persons in furtherance of a common intention, was premature and the petition would be dismissed with costs, HC observed. Thereafter, the petitioner sought permission to withdraw the petition.The petition alleged that, instead of registering a single FIR by arraying the three officers as accused, namely former Ponnani police SHO Vinod Valiyatoor, DSP V V Benny and former Malappuram SP S Sujith Das, police registered three separate FIRs on the premise that the alleged incidents were unconnected and standalone in nature, while omitting the names of the accused officials. The petitioner further alleged that the FIRs disclosed her identity, contrary to Section 72 of BNS.
Article Summary: High court dismisses as withdrawn a petition filed by the survivor in a sexual assault case against three senior police officers in Malappuram. The petition sought to cancel and re-register the FIR by including the officers’ names as accused instead of merely referring to their official positions.
